data.body:
[G3 child-coverage — container review of parent 1f19-adf2-ffb2-4b09 paired with THIS child (S4 30b3: Attested signing — pin verified_at_sha via the signed manifest channel); 4-bucket audit against the WHOLE EIGHT-child roster]

VERDICT: EMPTY G3 findings list. The union of the eight children (S1 6d56 materialize core, S2 70c7 cache, S2b b73c janitor, S3 fa49 gate threading, S4 30b3 THIS attested signing, S4b e308 claim-gate ref-coherence/ADR 0002, S5 b7fa CLI/MCP surfaces, S6 1ba5 docs) covers every parent acceptance/success criterion. No uncovered parent criterion is attributable to this pairing. Every absence below was cross-checked against the WHOLE roster before being dismissed.

WHAT THIS CHILD OWNS: the attested-signing slice — PRIMARY/SOLE owner of binding verified_at_sha into the signed bytes via the EXISTING manifest channel (a `verified-at-sha:<sha>` MANIFEST STEP, not a payload-schema change), plus the queryable field + lifecycle, the resolution boundary + fail-closed, the CAS-serialized atomic/idempotent/overwrite pin, the local-mode signing bar, and the in-toto envelope shape.

4-bucket coverage map (parent AC -> covering child/children; THIS child's role + bucket):
- AC1 (no code-reading gate reads the server working tree in attested mode; all read a faithful snapshot at the pinned SHA) — FULLY by S1 (materialize) + S3 (gate re-rooting). Bucket STRUCTURAL w.r.t. this child (Scope: "Out: snapshot/cache (S1/S2); gate parameter threading (S3)"); it signs over the pinned SHA, does not perform the read. Not an absence.
- AC2 (a signed verdict binds verified_at_sha via the signed MANIFEST CHANNEL — no payload-schema/version change, prior signatures still verify — reproducible against that SHA) — FULLY covered, THIS child PRIMARY OWNER: its AC1 (verified-at-sha:<sha> manifest step covered by the signature; NO _canonical_payload change, NO PAYLOAD_VERSION bump; regression test prior sigs verify; decision in ADR) + AC2 (queryable field; re-run against same SHA reproduces verdict+manifest over identical content; head_sha additive) + AC6 (in-toto envelope). Every clause of parent AC2 maps to a concrete AC here. Bucket FULLY. Heart of this child.
- AC3 (local mode reads in-place checkout AND cannot produce a signed verdict; source=local documented back-out) — SPLIT: THIS child owns the "CANNOT sign" half (its AC4: local/dirty run never sets verified_at_sha and never emits a signature); S3 (fa49) owns the "reads in-place checkout" + documented-back-out half; S6 docs. Clean reader/enforcer/doc split; no uncovered half. Bucket PARTIALLY (signing-bar half here).
- AC4 (concurrent gates on same/different SHAs without git-lock contention/cross-op corruption; cache + janitor reclaim under a defined free-space watermark (S2) without disrupting in-flight reads) — FULLY by S1 (atomic/no-worktree population) + S2 (single-flight, delete-on-last-close) + S2b (janitor watermark reclamation). This child's own AC3 (CAS-serialized pin + verified_at_sha write so two writers racing / a re-verify against a different SHA cannot interleave or leave a stale/double pin) is SIGNING-side concurrency safety, additive to — not substituting for — the git/cache concurrency of parent AC4. Bucket STRUCTURAL w.r.t. the git/cache half. Not an absence.
- AC5 (private-repo fetch failure surfaces a descriptive, actionable credential error) — FULLY by S1 (raises) + S6 (documents). This child's AC5 (unresolvable ref in attested mode fails closed, no signature) is the signing-side resolution boundary — adjacent to but distinct from the FETCH-credential error, which S1+S6 own. Bucket STRUCTURAL. Not an absence.
- AC6 (portable macOS+Linux: no external tar; no hardcoded /tmp; flock+mkdir fallback) — FULLY by S1 (impl) + S6 (docs). Bucket STRUCTURAL; out of this child's scope. Not an absence.
- AC7 (operational tunables — free-space watermark, default ref, grace/age windows — configurable with documented defaults, S2/S3) — FULLY by S2/S2b (watermark + grace/age) + S3 (default ref/source) + S6 (docs). This child consumes the default ref (origin/main) but does not own the tunable mechanism. Bucket STRUCTURAL. Not an absence.
- AC8 (an ADR captures the architecture + rejected alternatives — PID-lease, payload-schema SHA binding) — PARTIALLY co-owned: the PID+heartbeat-lease rejection + content-addressed layout + flock GC interlock + read-tree/checkout-index-over-git-archive are authored in S2 per the parent ADR note ("authored in S2/S4"); the **payload-schema SHA-binding** rejected alternative is PRECISELY this child's contribution — its AC1 records "the manifest-vs-payload signing decision ... in the architecture ADR." Both named rejected alternatives have an authoring home. (ADR 0002 — the code-drift ADR carried by S4b e308 — is a DISTINCT artifact, not the architecture ADR of parent AC8.) Bucket PARTIALLY (co-owner). Not an absence.
- AC9 (all EIGHT enumerated child stories closed; make lint + full test suite green) — STRUCTURAL/meta epic-completion; covered by the existence of all eight children (this is S4 of 8) + each child's green-bar obligation. Not an absence.

Success-criteria narrative ("Every code-reading gate verifies a client-specified, immutable, reproducible snapshot at a pinned SHA — never the server's mutable checkout — safely under concurrent distributed use; signed verdicts bind verified_at_sha via the manifest channel (reproducible, no prior-signature breakage); local mode preserves the single-developer verify-before-push workflow") decomposes onto AC1-AC3/AC8. The MIDDLE clause is verbatim this child's core deliverable (AC1/AC2). The "never the server's mutable checkout / immutable pinned-SHA basis" clause is S1/S3; "local preserves verify-before-push" is S3's in-place read + this child's signing bar (AC4). No uncovered obligation introduced by this child's existence.

Named-consumer / delegation cross-checks (covered-by-named-consumer, not absences):
- The claim-gate ref-resolution COHERENCE story is explicitly delegated OUT of this child to sibling S4b (e308) ("Claim-gate ref-resolution coherence is a sibling story ... Out: claim-gate ref-resolution coherence"). S4b is present in the roster and CLAIMS it (its AC1: plan-review file_impact hashing + claim-gate freshness re-check resolve through ONE shared boundary at the same pinned-SHA basis; AC2 negative test). The reproducibility sub-obligation of parent AC2 that depends on the bound dependency map and the later freshness re-check hashing at the SAME basis is therefore covered by a named sibling — not orphaned.
- HMAC-trust-model + in-toto-shape DOCS delegated to S6 ("limits documented in S6"); S6 AC4 claims it.
- snapshot/cache (S1/S2) delegated out and owned by S1/S2/S2b.

ANTI-FP cross-check on the one plausible absence: could parent AC2's "reproducible against that SHA" be left UNCOVERED because this child binds the SHA in the manifest but the verdict body / dependency hashes might still be computed against the mutable checkout? Checked the WHOLE roster — NO: the snapshot-only read basis is claimed by S1+S3 (AC1 re-rooting), and the residual attestation-hashing/ref-coherence path is now explicitly claimed by S4b (AC1) with a negative test guarding whole-HEAD divergence. The obligation is claimed across the roster; the only open question (which module the routing physically lands in / WHO threads the snapshot handle into plan_review/attest.py) is the standing G4 dependency-gap concern on this ticket — a CONSISTENCY issue, NOT a G3 union-coverage absence, since a criterion claimed by a child is covered for G3 purposes.

Conclusion: this child cleanly covers the attested-signing slice — PRIMARY OWNER of parent AC2, the "cannot-sign" half of AC3, and the payload-schema-SHA-binding rejected-alternative clause of AC8, contributing its own signing-side concurrency safety (CAS pin) and fail-closed resolution boundary. Every parent acceptance/success criterion has a named owner in the eight-story roster; none is left uncovered. NO G3 finding for this pairing.
